text: When you're learning to code, bugs are a normal part of the process. Identifying bugs is the first step: notice when your program doesn't behave as expected. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently. Instead of guessing, break your code into smaller parts and test each one. Debugging tools like breakpoints or step-through execution let you pause your program and check variable values. Another useful strategy is logging, where you print messages to see what your code is doing at different stages. By combining these tools and a methodical mindset, you can fix errors more quickly and become a better programmer.
